(坂出市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675600}} == Japanese content == {{座標一覧}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} recorded as "Kamo Jinja" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}{{ill|lt=Aya District|Q11658001|WD=Q11658001|1=Aya district}}. In {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, there are the following two candidate shrines ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}). # {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)#Higashi Kamo Shrine|Q11675600|WD=Q11675600|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)}} (992 Kamo-chō, {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}) - former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. # {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)#Nishi Kamo Shrine|Q11675600|WD=Q11675600|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)}} (540 Kamo-chō, Sakaide, Kagawa Prefecture) - former village shrine. === Overview === Kamo Shrine as a Shikinaisha is listed in the ''Engishiki'' register and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'' as having been granted court rank. The area preserves the old toponym of Kamo-be-go in Aya District in ancient times''Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', section on Kamo Village.. Today there are two shrines, east and west, and there is no established consensus as to which one should be identified as the Engishiki shrine''Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', section on Kamo Shrine.. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 7 ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}), 10th month 9th day: promoted from junior fifth rank lower to junior fifth rank upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Kamo-no-kami" * Jōgan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}), 5th month 27th day: senior fifth rank lower (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Kamo Tenjin" === Higashi Kamo Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Higashi Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:東鴨神社 拝殿.JPG|250px]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Mikoto|1=Hitokotonushi}} (ひとことぬしのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Tamayoribime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} (たまよりびめのみこと) === History === It is enshrined on the slope of a hill standing east of the Kamosho area. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called names such as "Katsuragi Daimyojin," "Katsuragi no Yashiro," and "Kamosho Daimyojin". According to the shrine-held ''Katsuragi Daimyojin-ki'', Atto Ōtaru asked a priest of the Kyoto {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine|Q1306606|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines|1=Kamo shrines}} during the {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}-{{ill|lt=824|Q30353|WD=Q30353|1=824}}), and invited {{ill|lt=Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295|WD=Q11673295|lt=Takakamo no Kami|1=Takakamo Shrine}} from Katsuragi in Yamato, making it the tutelary deity of Kamosho. === Precinct === ファイル:東鴨神社 本殿.JPG|Main hall ファイル:東鴨神社 正面.JPG|Front view ファイル:東鴨神社 鳥居.JPG|Torii === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kamo-chō 992 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kamogawa Station|Q5115423|WD=Q5115423|1=Kamogawa Station}} (about 16 minutes on foot) === Nishi Kamo Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Nishi Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:西鴨神社 拝殿.JPG|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}Kamo-chō 540 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|18|21.99|N|133|54|37.81|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=813|Q30360|WD=Q30360|1=813}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = Godo Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Nishi Kamo Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|18|21.99|N|133|54|37.81|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|18|21.99|N|133|54|37.81|E}}}} }} '''Nishi Kamo Shrine''' (にしかもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} (わけいかづちのみこと) === History === It is enshrined southwest outside the Kamosho area. Tradition says it was the overall guardian shrine of both Kamo and Uji-be villages since its foundation and was popularly called "Kamo no Ōmori". Its foundation is said to date to {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=813|Q30360|WD=Q30360|1=813}}), when Atto Ōtaru invited the deity from Yamato Province. The shrine buildings later fell into ruin, but in the {{ill|lt=Chōryaku|Q1193519|WD=Q1193519|1=Chōryaku}} era ({{ill|lt=1037|Q19335|WD=Q19335|1=1037}}-{{ill|lt=1040|Q19342|WD=Q19342|1=1040}}), shrine family member Irie Minbu Kageaki petitioned in Kyoto and restored it. The shrine still preserves a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} board on which the characters for Chōryaku and Kageaki can actually be confirmed. Subsequent transitions are unclear. It is said to have burned in warfare during the Sengoku period, and a temporary hall was restored in {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1584|Q6728|WD=Q6728|1=1584}}). This munafuda reads "Kamo Sansho Daimyojin" and includes "in ancient times each of the three shrines had its own place". Later, construction and repairs of shrine buildings were carried out in the Edo period. === Precinct === ファイル:西鴨神社 本殿.JPG|Main hall ファイル:西鴨神社 正面.JPG|Front view ファイル:西鴨神社 鳥居.JPG|Torii === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kamo-chō 540 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kamogawa Station|Q5115423|WD=Q5115423|1=Kamogawa Station}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Sakaide City section, Kamo Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもしんしやさかいてし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[category:坂出市の歴史]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧村社]] [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:坂出市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

(坂出市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675600}} == Japanese content == {{座標一覧}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} recorded as "Kamo Jinja" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}{{ill|lt=Aya District|Q11658001|WD=Q11658001|1=Aya district}}. In {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, there are the following two candidate shrines ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}). # {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)#Higashi Kamo Shrine|Q11675600|WD=Q11675600|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)}} (992 Kamo-chō, {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}) - former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. # {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)#Nishi Kamo Shrine|Q11675600|WD=Q11675600|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)}} (540 Kamo-chō, Sakaide, Kagawa Prefecture) - former village shrine. === Overview === Kamo Shrine as a Shikinaisha is listed in the ''Engishiki'' register and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'' as having been granted court rank. The area preserves the old toponym of Kamo-be-go in Aya District in ancient times''Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', section on Kamo Village.. Today there are two shrines, east and west, and there is no established consensus as to which one should be identified as the Engishiki shrine''Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', section on Kamo Shrine.. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 7 ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}), 10th month 9th day: promoted from junior fifth rank lower to junior fifth rank upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Kamo-no-kami" * Jōgan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}), 5th month 27th day: senior fifth rank lower (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Kamo Tenjin" === Higashi Kamo Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Higashi Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:東鴨神社 拝殿.JPG|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}Kamo-chō 992 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|18|43.73|N|133|54|56.42|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Mikoto|1=Hitokotonushi}}

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Mikoto|1=Hitokotonushi}} (ひとことぬしのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Tamayoribime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} (たまよりびめのみこと) === History === It is enshrined on the slope of a hill standing east of the Kamosho area. According to the shrine-held ''Katsuragi Daimyojin-ki'', Atto Ōtaru asked a priest of the Kyoto {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine|Q1306606|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines|1=Kamo shrines}} during the {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}-{{ill|lt=824|Q30353|WD=Q30353|1=824}}), and invited {{ill|lt=Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295|WD=Q11673295|lt=Takakamo no Kami|1=Takakamo Shrine}} from Katsuragi in Yamato, making it the tutelary deity of Kamosho. === Precinct === ファイル:東鴨神社 本殿.JPG|Main hall ファイル:東鴨神社 正面.JPG|Front view ファイル:東鴨神社 鳥居.JPG|Torii === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kamo-chō 992 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kamogawa Station|Q5115423|WD=Q5115423|1=Kamogawa Station}} (about 16 minutes on foot) === Nishi Kamo Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Nishi Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:西鴨神社 拝殿.JPG|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}Kamo-chō 540 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|18|21.99|N|133|54|37.81|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=813|Q30360|WD=Q30360|1=813}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = Godo Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Nishi Kamo Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|18|21.99|N|133|54|37.81|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|18|21.99|N|133|54|37.81|E}}}} }} '''Nishi Kamo Shrine''' (にしかもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} (わけいかづちのみこと) === History === It is enshrined southwest outside the Kamosho area. Tradition says it was the overall guardian shrine of both Kamo and Uji-be villages since its foundation and was popularly called "Kamo no Ōmori". Its foundation is said to date to {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=813|Q30360|WD=Q30360|1=813}}), when Atto Ōtaru invited the deity from Yamato Province. The shrine buildings later fell into ruin, but in the {{ill|lt=Chōryaku|Q1193519|WD=Q1193519|1=Chōryaku}} era ({{ill|lt=1037|Q19335|WD=Q19335|1=1037}}-{{ill|lt=1040|Q19342|WD=Q19342|1=1040}}), shrine family member Irie Minbu Kageaki petitioned in Kyoto and restored it. The shrine still preserves a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} board on which the characters for Chōryaku and Kageaki can actually be confirmed. Subsequent transitions are unclear. It is said to have burned in warfare during the Sengoku period, and a temporary hall was restored in {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1584|Q6728|WD=Q6728|1=1584}}). This munafuda reads "Kamo Sansho Daimyojin" and includes "in ancient times each of the three shrines had its own place". Later, construction and repairs of shrine buildings were carried out in the Edo period. === Precinct === ファイル:西鴨神社 本殿.JPG|Main hall ファイル:西鴨神社 正面.JPG|Front view ファイル:西鴨神社 鳥居.JPG|Torii === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kamo-chō 540 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kamogawa Station|Q5115423|WD=Q5115423|1=Kamogawa Station}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Sakaide City section, Kamo Shrine entry {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[category:坂出市の歴史]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧村社]] [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:坂出市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

(坂出市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675600}} == Japanese content == {{座標一覧}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} recorded as "Kamo Jinja" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}{{ill|lt=Aya District|Q11658001|WD=Q11658001|1=Aya district}}. In {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, there are the following two candidate shrines ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}). # {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)#Higashi Kamo Shrine|Q11675600|WD=Q11675600|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)}} (992 Kamo-chō, {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}) - former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. # {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)#Nishi Kamo Shrine|Q11675600|WD=Q11675600|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)}} (540 Kamo-chō, Sakaide, Kagawa Prefecture) - former village shrine. === Overview === Kamo Shrine as a Shikinaisha is listed in the ''Engishiki'' register and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'' as having been granted court rank. The area preserves the old toponym of Kamo-be-go in Aya District in ancient times''Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', section on Kamo Village.. Today there are two shrines, east and west, and there is no established consensus as to which one should be identified as the Engishiki shrine''Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', section on Kamo Shrine.. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 7 ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}), 10th month 9th day: promoted from junior fifth rank lower to junior fifth rank upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Kamo-no-kami" * Jōgan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}), 5th month 27th day: senior fifth rank lower (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Kamo Tenjin" === Higashi Kamo Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Higashi Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:東鴨神社 拝殿.JPG|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}Kamo-chō 992 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|18|43.73|N|133|54|56.42|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Mikoto|1=Hitokotonushi}}
{{ill|lt=Tamayoribime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (tradition) during {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}-{{ill|lt=824|Q30353|WD=Q30353|1=824}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Higashi Kamo Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|18|43.73|N|133|54|56.42|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|18|43.73|N|133|54|56.42|E}}}} }} '''Higashi Kamo Shrine''' (ひがしかもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Mikoto|1=Hitokotonushi}} (ひとことぬしのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Tamayoribime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} (たまよりびめのみこと) === History === It is enshrined on the slope of a hill standing east of the Kamosho area. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called names such as "Katsuragi Daimyojin," "Katsuragi no Yashiro," and "Kamosho Daimyojin". According to the shrine-held ''Katsuragi Daimyojin-ki'', Atto Ōtaru asked a priest of the Kyoto {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine|Q1306606|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines|1=Kamo shrines}} during the {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}-{{ill|lt=824|Q30353|WD=Q30353|1=824}}), and invited {{ill|lt=Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295|WD=Q11673295|lt=Takakamo no Kami|1=Takakamo Shrine}} from Katsuragi in Yamato, making it the tutelary deity of Kamosho. === Precinct === ファイル:東鴨神社 本殿.JPG|Main hall ファイル:東鴨神社 正面.JPG|Front view ファイル:東鴨神社 鳥居.JPG|Torii === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kamo-chō 992 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kamogawa Station|Q5115423|WD=Q5115423|1=Kamogawa Station}} (about 16 minutes on foot) === Nishi Kamo Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Nishi Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:西鴨神社 拝殿.JPG|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}Kamo-chō 540 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|18|21.99|N|133|54|37.81|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=813|Q30360|WD=Q30360|1=813}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = Godo Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Nishi Kamo Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|18|21.99|N|133|54|37.81|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|18|21.99|N|133|54|37.81|E}}}} }} '''Nishi Kamo Shrine''' (にしかもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} (わけいかづちのみこと) === History === It is enshrined southwest outside the Kamosho area. Tradition says it was the overall guardian shrine of both Kamo and Uji-be villages since its foundation and was popularly called "Kamo no Ōmori". Its foundation is said to date to {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=813|Q30360|WD=Q30360|1=813}}), when Atto Ōtaru invited the deity from Yamato Province. The shrine buildings later fell into ruin, but in the {{ill|lt=Chōryaku|Q1193519|WD=Q1193519|1=Chōryaku}} era ({{ill|lt=1037|Q19335|WD=Q19335|1=1037}}-{{ill|lt=1040|Q19342|WD=Q19342|1=1040}}), shrine family member Irie Minbu Kageaki petitioned in Kyoto and restored it. The shrine still preserves a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} board on which the characters for Chōryaku and Kageaki can actually be confirmed. Subsequent transitions are unclear. It is said to have burned in warfare during the Sengoku period, and a temporary hall was restored in {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1584|Q6728|WD=Q6728|1=1584}}). This munafuda reads "Kamo Sansho Daimyojin" and includes "in ancient times each of the three shrines had its own place". Later, construction and repairs of shrine buildings were carried out in the Edo period. === Precinct === ファイル:西鴨神社 本殿.JPG|Main hall ファイル:西鴨神社 正面.JPG|Front view ファイル:西鴨神社 鳥居.JPG|Torii === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kamo-chō 540 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kamogawa Station|Q5115423|WD=Q5115423|1=Kamogawa Station}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Sakaide City section, Kamo Shrine entry {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[category:坂出市の歴史]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:坂出市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

(坂出市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675600}} == Japanese content == {{座標一覧}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} recorded as "Kamo Jinja" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}{{ill|lt=Aya District|Q11658001|WD=Q11658001|1=Aya district}}. In {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, there are the following two candidate shrines ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}). # {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)#Higashi Kamo Shrine|Q11675600|WD=Q11675600|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)}} (992 Kamo-chō, {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}) - former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. # {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)#Nishi Kamo Shrine|Q11675600|WD=Q11675600|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)}} (540 Kamo-chō, Sakaide, Kagawa Prefecture) - former village shrine. === Overview === Kamo Shrine as a Shikinaisha is listed in the ''Engishiki'' register and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'' as having been granted court rank. The area preserves the old toponym of Kamo-be-go in Aya District in ancient times''Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', section on Kamo Village.. Today there are two shrines, east and west, and there is no established consensus as to which one should be identified as the Engishiki shrine''Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', section on Kamo Shrine.. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 7 ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}), 10th month 9th day: promoted from junior fifth rank lower to junior fifth rank upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Kamo-no-kami" * Jōgan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}), 5th month 27th day: senior fifth rank lower (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Kamo Tenjin" === Higashi Kamo Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Higashi Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:東鴨神社 拝殿.JPG|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}Kamo-chō 992 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|18|43.73|N|133|54|56.42|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Mikoto|1=Hitokotonushi}}
{{ill|lt=Tamayoribime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (tradition) during {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}-{{ill|lt=824|Q30353|WD=Q30353|1=824}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Higashi Kamo Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|18|43.73|N|133|54|56.42|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|18|43.73|N|133|54|56.42|E}}}} }} '''Higashi Kamo Shrine''' (ひがしかもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Mikoto|1=Hitokotonushi}} (ひとことぬしのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Tamayoribime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} (たまよりびめのみこと) === History === It is enshrined on the slope of a hill standing east of the Kamosho area. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called names such as "Katsuragi Daimyojin," "Katsuragi no Yashiro," and "Kamosho Daimyojin". According to the shrine-held ''Katsuragi Daimyojin-ki'', Atto Ōtaru asked a priest of the Kyoto {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine|Q1306606|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines|1=Kamo shrines}} during the {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}-{{ill|lt=824|Q30353|WD=Q30353|1=824}}), and invited {{ill|lt=Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295|WD=Q11673295|lt=Takakamo no Kami|1=Takakamo Shrine}} from Katsuragi in Yamato, making it the tutelary deity of Kamosho. === Precinct === ファイル:東鴨神社 本殿.JPG|Main hall ファイル:東鴨神社 正面.JPG|Front view ファイル:東鴨神社 鳥居.JPG|Torii === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kamo-chō 992 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kamogawa Station|Q5115423|WD=Q5115423|1=Kamogawa Station}} (about 16 minutes on foot) === Nishi Kamo Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Nishi Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:西鴨神社 拝殿.JPG|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}Kamo-chō 540 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|18|21.99|N|133|54|37.81|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}

Tradition says it was the overall guardian shrine of both Kamo and Uji-be villages since its foundation and was popularly called "Kamo no Ōmori". Its foundation is said to date to {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=813|Q30360|WD=Q30360|1=813}}), when Atto Ōtaru invited the deity from Yamato Province. The shrine buildings later fell into ruin, but in the {{ill|lt=Chōryaku|Q1193519|WD=Q1193519|1=Chōryaku}} era ({{ill|lt=1037|Q19335|WD=Q19335|1=1037}}-{{ill|lt=1040|Q19342|WD=Q19342|1=1040}}), shrine family member Irie Minbu Kageaki petitioned in Kyoto and restored it. The shrine still preserves a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} board on which the characters for Chōryaku and Kageaki can actually be confirmed. Subsequent transitions are unclear. It is said to have burned in warfare during the Sengoku period, and a temporary hall was restored in {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1584|Q6728|WD=Q6728|1=1584}}). This munafuda reads "Kamo Sansho Daimyojin" and includes "in ancient times each of the three shrines had its own place". Later, construction and repairs of shrine buildings were carried out in the Edo period. === Precinct === ファイル:西鴨神社 本殿.JPG|Main hall ファイル:西鴨神社 正面.JPG|Front view ファイル:西鴨神社 鳥居.JPG|Torii === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kamo-chō 540 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kamogawa Station|Q5115423|WD=Q5115423|1=Kamogawa Station}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Sakaide City section, Kamo Shrine entry {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[category:坂出市の歴史]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakaide, Kagawa]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

(坂出市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675600}} == Japanese content == {{座標一覧}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} recorded as "Kamo Jinja" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}{{ill|lt=Aya District|Q11658001|WD=Q11658001|1=Aya district}}. In {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, there are the following two candidate shrines ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}). # {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)#Higashi Kamo Shrine|Q11675600|WD=Q11675600|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)}} (992 Kamo-chō, {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}) - former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. # {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)#Nishi Kamo Shrine|Q11675600|WD=Q11675600|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)}} (540 Kamo-chō, Sakaide, Kagawa Prefecture) - former village shrine. === Overview === Kamo Shrine as a Shikinaisha is listed in the ''Engishiki'' register and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'' as having been granted court rank. The area preserves the old toponym of Kamo-be-go in Aya District in ancient times''Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', section on Kamo Village.. Today there are two shrines, east and west, and there is no established consensus as to which one should be identified as the Engishiki shrine''Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', section on Kamo Shrine.. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 7 ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}), 10th month 9th day: promoted from junior fifth rank lower to junior fifth rank upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Kamo-no-kami" * Jōgan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}), 5th month 27th day: senior fifth rank lower (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Kamo Tenjin" === Higashi Kamo Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Higashi Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:東鴨神社 拝殿.JPG|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}Kamo-chō 992 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|18|43.73|N|133|54|56.42|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Mikoto|1=Hitokotonushi}}
{{ill|lt=Tamayoribime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (tradition) during {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}-{{ill|lt=824|Q30353|WD=Q30353|1=824}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Higashi Kamo Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|18|43.73|N|133|54|56.42|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|18|43.73|N|133|54|56.42|E}}}} }} '''Higashi Kamo Shrine''' (ひがしかもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Mikoto|1=Hitokotonushi}} (ひとことぬしのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Tamayoribime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} (たまよりびめのみこと) === History === It is enshrined on the slope of a hill standing east of the Kamosho area. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called names such as "Katsuragi Daimyojin," "Katsuragi no Yashiro," and "Kamosho Daimyojin". According to the shrine-held ''Katsuragi Daimyojin-ki'', Atto Ōtaru asked a priest of the Kyoto {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine|Q1306606|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines|1=Kamo shrines}} during the {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}-{{ill|lt=824|Q30353|WD=Q30353|1=824}}), and invited {{ill|lt=Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295|WD=Q11673295|lt=Takakamo no Kami|1=Takakamo Shrine}} from Katsuragi in Yamato, making it the tutelary deity of Kamosho. === Precinct === ファイル:東鴨神社 本殿.JPG|Main hall ファイル:東鴨神社 正面.JPG|Front view ファイル:東鴨神社 鳥居.JPG|Torii === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kamo-chō 992 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kamogawa Station|Q5115423|WD=Q5115423|1=Kamogawa Station}} (about 16 minutes on foot) === Nishi Kamo Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Nishi Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:西鴨神社 拝殿.JPG|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}Kamo-chō 540 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|18|21.99|N|133|54|37.81|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=813|Q30360|WD=Q30360|1=813}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = Godo Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Nishi Kamo Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|18|21.99|N|133|54|37.81|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|18|21.99|N|133|54|37.81|E}}}} }} '''Nishi Kamo Shrine''' (にしかもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} (わけいかづちのみこと) === History === It is enshrined southwest outside the Kamosho area. Tradition says it was the overall guardian shrine of both Kamo and Uji-be villages since its foundation and was popularly called "Kamo no Ōmori". Its foundation is said to date to {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=813|Q30360|WD=Q30360|1=813}}), when Atto Ōtaru invited the deity from Yamato Province. The shrine buildings later fell into ruin, but in the {{ill|lt=Chōryaku|Q1193519|WD=Q1193519|1=Chōryaku}} era ({{ill|lt=1037|Q19335|WD=Q19335|1=1037}}-{{ill|lt=1040|Q19342|WD=Q19342|1=1040}}), shrine family member Irie Minbu Kageaki petitioned in Kyoto and restored it. The shrine still preserves a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} board on which the characters for Chōryaku and Kageaki can actually be confirmed. Subsequent transitions are unclear. It is said to have burned in warfare during the Sengoku period, and a temporary hall was restored in {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1584|Q6728|WD=Q6728|1=1584}}). This munafuda reads "Kamo Sansho Daimyojin" and includes "in ancient times each of the three shrines had its own place". Later, construction and repairs of shrine buildings were carried out in the Edo period. === Precinct === ファイル:西鴨神社 本殿.JPG|Main hall ファイル:西鴨神社 正面.JPG|Front view ファイル:西鴨神社 鳥居.JPG|Torii === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kamo-chō 540 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kamogawa Station|Q5115423|WD=Q5115423|1=Kamogawa Station}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Sakaide City section, Kamo Shrine entry {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sakaide]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakaide, Kagawa]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

(坂出市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675600}} == Japanese content == {{座標一覧}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} recorded as "Kamo Jinja" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}{{ill|lt=Aya District|Q11658001|WD=Q11658001|1=Aya district}}. In {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, there are the following two candidate shrines ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}). # {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)#Higashi Kamo Shrine|Q11675600|WD=Q11675600|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)}} (992 Kamo-chō, {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}) - former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. # {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)#Nishi Kamo Shrine|Q11675600|WD=Q11675600|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)}} (540 Kamo-chō, Sakaide, Kagawa Prefecture) - former village shrine. === Overview === Kamo Shrine as a Shikinaisha is listed in the ''Engishiki'' register and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'' as having been granted court rank. The area preserves the old toponym of Kamo-be-go in Aya District in ancient times''Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', section on Kamo Village.. Today there are two shrines, east and west, and there is no established consensus as to which one should be identified as the Engishiki shrine''Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', section on Kamo Shrine.. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 7 ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}), 10th month 9th day: promoted from junior fifth rank lower to junior fifth rank upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Kamo-no-kami" * Jōgan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}), 5th month 27th day: senior fifth rank lower (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Kamo Tenjin" === Higashi Kamo Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Higashi Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:東鴨神社 拝殿.JPG|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}Kamo-chō 992 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|18|43.73|N|133|54|56.42|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Higashi Kamo Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Mikoto|1=Hitokotonushi}}
{{ill|lt=Tamayoribime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (tradition) during {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}-{{ill|lt=824|Q30353|WD=Q30353|1=824}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Higashi Kamo Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|18|43.73|N|133|54|56.42|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|18|43.73|N|133|54|56.42|E}}}} }} '''Higashi Kamo Shrine''' (ひがしかもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Mikoto|1=Hitokotonushi}} (ひとことぬしのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Tamayoribime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} (たまよりびめのみこと) === History === It is enshrined on the slope of a hill standing east of the Kamosho area. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called names such as "Katsuragi Daimyojin," "Katsuragi no Yashiro," and "Kamosho Daimyojin". According to the shrine-held ''Katsuragi Daimyojin-ki'', Atto Ōtaru asked a priest of the Kyoto {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine|Q1306606|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines|1=Kamo shrines}} during the {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}-{{ill|lt=824|Q30353|WD=Q30353|1=824}}), and invited {{ill|lt=Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295|WD=Q11673295|lt=Takakamo no Kami|1=Takakamo Shrine}} from Katsuragi in Yamato, making it the tutelary deity of Kamosho. === Precinct === ファイル:東鴨神社 本殿.JPG|Main hall ファイル:東鴨神社 正面.JPG|Front view ファイル:東鴨神社 鳥居.JPG|Torii === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kamo-chō 992 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kamogawa Station|Q5115423|WD=Q5115423|1=Kamogawa Station}} (about 16 minutes on foot) === Nishi Kamo Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Nishi Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:西鴨神社 拝殿.JPG|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}Kamo-chō 540 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|18|21.99|N|133|54|37.81|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nishi Kamo Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=813|Q30360|WD=Q30360|1=813}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = Godo Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Nishi Kamo Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|18|21.99|N|133|54|37.81|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|18|21.99|N|133|54|37.81|E}}}} }} '''Nishi Kamo Shrine''' (にしかもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} (わけいかづちのみこと) === History === It is enshrined southwest outside the Kamosho area. Tradition says it was the overall guardian shrine of both Kamo and Uji-be villages since its foundation and was popularly called "Kamo no Ōmori". Its foundation is said to date to {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=813|Q30360|WD=Q30360|1=813}}), when Atto Ōtaru invited the deity from Yamato Province. The shrine buildings later fell into ruin, but in the {{ill|lt=Chōryaku|Q1193519|WD=Q1193519|1=Chōryaku}} era ({{ill|lt=1037|Q19335|WD=Q19335|1=1037}}-{{ill|lt=1040|Q19342|WD=Q19342|1=1040}}), shrine family member Irie Minbu Kageaki petitioned in Kyoto and restored it. The shrine still preserves a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} board on which the characters for Chōryaku and Kageaki can actually be confirmed. Subsequent transitions are unclear. It is said to have burned in warfare during the Sengoku period, and a temporary hall was restored in {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1584|Q6728|WD=Q6728|1=1584}}). This munafuda reads "Kamo Sansho Daimyojin" and includes "in ancient times each of the three shrines had its own place". Later, construction and repairs of shrine buildings were carried out in the Edo period. === Precinct === ファイル:西鴨神社 本殿.JPG|Main hall ファイル:西鴨神社 正面.JPG|Front view ファイル:西鴨神社 鳥居.JPG|Torii === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kamo-chō 540 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kamogawa Station|Q5115423|WD=Q5115423|1=Kamogawa Station}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Sakaide City section, Kamo Shrine entry {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sakaide]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakaide, Kagawa]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

(坂出市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675600}} == Japanese content == {{座標一覧}} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} recorded as "Kamo Jinja" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}{{ill|lt=Aya District|Q11658001|WD=Q11658001|1=Aya district}}. In {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, there are the following two candidate shrines ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}). # {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)#Higashi Kamo Shrine|Q11675600|WD=Q11675600|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)}} (992 Kamo-chō, {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}) - former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. # {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)#Nishi Kamo Shrine|Q11675600|WD=Q11675600|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)}} (540 Kamo-chō, Sakaide, Kagawa Prefecture) - former village shrine. === Overview === Kamo Shrine as a Shikinaisha is listed in the ''Engishiki'' register and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'' as having been granted court rank. The area preserves the old toponym of Kamo-be-go in Aya District in ancient times''Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', section on Kamo Village.. Today there are two shrines, east and west, and there is no established consensus as to which one should be identified as the Engishiki shrine''Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', section on Kamo Shrine.. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 7 ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}), 10th month 9th day: promoted from junior fifth rank lower to junior fifth rank upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Kamo-no-kami" * Jōgan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}), 5th month 27th day: senior fifth rank lower (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Kamo Tenjin" === Higashi Kamo Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Higashi Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:東鴨神社 拝殿.JPG|250px]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (tradition) during {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}-{{ill|lt=824|Q30353|WD=Q30353|1=824}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Higashi Kamo Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|18|43.73|N|133|54|56.42|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|18|43.73|N|133|54|56.42|E}}}} }} '''Higashi Kamo Shrine''' (ひがしかもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Mikoto|1=Hitokotonushi}} (ひとことぬしのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Tamayoribime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} (たまよりびめのみこと) === History === It is enshrined on the slope of a hill standing east of the Kamosho area. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called names such as "Katsuragi Daimyojin," "Katsuragi no Yashiro," and "Kamosho Daimyojin". According to the shrine-held ''Katsuragi Daimyojin-ki'', Atto Ōtaru asked a priest of the Kyoto {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine|Q1306606|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines|1=Kamo shrines}} during the {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}-{{ill|lt=824|Q30353|WD=Q30353|1=824}}), and invited {{ill|lt=Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295|WD=Q11673295|lt=Takakamo no Kami|1=Takakamo Shrine}} from Katsuragi in Yamato, making it the tutelary deity of Kamosho. === Precinct === ファイル:東鴨神社 本殿.JPG|Main hall ファイル:東鴨神社 正面.JPG|Front view ファイル:東鴨神社 鳥居.JPG|Torii === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kamo-chō 992 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kamogawa Station|Q5115423|WD=Q5115423|1=Kamogawa Station}} (about 16 minutes on foot) === Nishi Kamo Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Nishi Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:西鴨神社 拝殿.JPG|250px]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=813|Q30360|WD=Q30360|1=813}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = Godo Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Nishi Kamo Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|18|21.99|N|133|54|37.81|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|18|21.99|N|133|54|37.81|E}}}} }} '''Nishi Kamo Shrine''' (にしかもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} (わけいかづちのみこと) === History === It is enshrined southwest outside the Kamosho area. Tradition says it was the overall guardian shrine of both Kamo and Uji-be villages since its foundation and was popularly called "Kamo no Ōmori". Its foundation is said to date to {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=813|Q30360|WD=Q30360|1=813}}), when Atto Ōtaru invited the deity from Yamato Province. The shrine buildings later fell into ruin, but in the {{ill|lt=Chōryaku|Q1193519|WD=Q1193519|1=Chōryaku}} era ({{ill|lt=1037|Q19335|WD=Q19335|1=1037}}-{{ill|lt=1040|Q19342|WD=Q19342|1=1040}}), shrine family member Irie Minbu Kageaki petitioned in Kyoto and restored it. The shrine still preserves a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} board on which the characters for Chōryaku and Kageaki can actually be confirmed. Subsequent transitions are unclear. It is said to have burned in warfare during the Sengoku period, and a temporary hall was restored in {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1584|Q6728|WD=Q6728|1=1584}}). This munafuda reads "Kamo Sansho Daimyojin" and includes "in ancient times each of the three shrines had its own place". Later, construction and repairs of shrine buildings were carried out in the Edo period. === Precinct === ファイル:西鴨神社 本殿.JPG|Main hall ファイル:西鴨神社 正面.JPG|Front view ファイル:西鴨神社 鳥居.JPG|Torii === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kamo-chō 540 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kamogawa Station|Q5115423|WD=Q5115423|1=Kamogawa Station}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Sakaide City section, Kamo Shrine entry {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sakaide]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakaide, Kagawa]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

In {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, there are the following two candidate shrines ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}). # {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)#Higashi Kamo Shrine|Q11675600|WD=Q11675600|lt=Higashi Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)}} (992 Kamo-chō, {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}) - former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. # {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)#Nishi Kamo Shrine|Q11675600|WD=Q11675600|lt=Nishi Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)}} (540 Kamo-chō, Sakaide, Kagawa Prefecture) - former village shrine. === Overview === Kamo Shrine as a Shikinaisha is listed in the ''Engishiki'' register and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'' as having been granted court rank. The area preserves the old toponym of Kamo-be-go in Aya District in ancient times''Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', section on Kamo Village.. Today there are two shrines, east and west, and there is no established consensus as to which one should be identified as the Engishiki shrine''Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', section on Kamo Shrine.. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 7 ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}), 10th month 9th day: promoted from junior fifth rank lower to junior fifth rank upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Kamo-no-kami" * Jōgan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}), 5th month 27th day: senior fifth rank lower (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Kamo Tenjin" === Higashi Kamo Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Higashi Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:東鴨神社 拝殿.JPG|250px]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Mikoto|1=Hitokotonushi}} (ひとことぬしのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Tamayoribime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} (たまよりびめのみこと) === History === It is enshrined on the slope of a hill standing east of the Kamosho area. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called names such as "Katsuragi Daimyojin," "Katsuragi no Yashiro," and "Kamosho Daimyojin". According to the shrine-held ''Katsuragi Daimyojin-ki'', Atto Ōtaru asked a priest of the Kyoto {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine|Q1306606|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines|1=Kamo shrines}} during the {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}-{{ill|lt=824|Q30353|WD=Q30353|1=824}}), and invited {{ill|lt=Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295|WD=Q11673295|lt=Takakamo no Kami|1=Takakamo Shrine}} from Katsuragi in Yamato, making it the tutelary deity of Kamosho. === Precinct === ファイル:東鴨神社 本殿.JPG|Main hall ファイル:東鴨神社 正面.JPG|Front view ファイル:東鴨神社 鳥居.JPG|Torii === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kamo-chō 992 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kamogawa Station|Q5115423|WD=Q5115423|1=Kamogawa Station}} (about 16 minutes on foot) === Nishi Kamo Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Nishi Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:西鴨神社 拝殿.JPG|250px]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=813|Q30360|WD=Q30360|1=813}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = Godo Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Nishi Kamo Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|18|21.99|N|133|54|37.81|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|18|21.99|N|133|54|37.81|E}}}} }} '''Nishi Kamo Shrine''' (にしかもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} (わけいかづちのみこと) === History === It is enshrined southwest outside the Kamosho area. Tradition says it was the overall guardian shrine of both Kamo and Uji-be villages since its foundation and was popularly called "Kamo no Ōmori". Its foundation is said to date to {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=813|Q30360|WD=Q30360|1=813}}), when Atto Ōtaru invited the deity from Yamato Province. The shrine buildings later fell into ruin, but in the {{ill|lt=Chōryaku|Q1193519|WD=Q1193519|1=Chōryaku}} era ({{ill|lt=1037|Q19335|WD=Q19335|1=1037}}-{{ill|lt=1040|Q19342|WD=Q19342|1=1040}}), shrine family member Irie Minbu Kageaki petitioned in Kyoto and restored it. The shrine still preserves a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} board on which the characters for Chōryaku and Kageaki can actually be confirmed. Subsequent transitions are unclear. It is said to have burned in warfare during the Sengoku period, and a temporary hall was restored in {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1584|Q6728|WD=Q6728|1=1584}}). This munafuda reads "Kamo Sansho Daimyojin" and includes "in ancient times each of the three shrines had its own place". Later, construction and repairs of shrine buildings were carried out in the Edo period. === Precinct === ファイル:西鴨神社 本殿.JPG|Main hall ファイル:西鴨神社 正面.JPG|Front view ファイル:西鴨神社 鳥居.JPG|Torii === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kamo-chō 540 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kamogawa Station|Q5115423|WD=Q5115423|1=Kamogawa Station}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Sakaide City section, Kamo Shrine entry {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Sakaide)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sakaide]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakaide, Kagawa]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Mikoto|1=Hitokotonushi}} (ひとことぬしのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Tamayoribime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} (たまよりびめのみこと) === History === It is enshrined on the slope of a hill standing east of the Kamosho area. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called names such as "Katsuragi Daimyojin," "Katsuragi no Yashiro," and "Kamosho Daimyojin". According to the shrine-held ''Katsuragi Daimyojin-ki'', Atto Ōtaru asked a priest of the Kyoto {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine|Q1306606|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrines|1=Kamo shrines}} during the {{ill|lt=Konin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}-{{ill|lt=824|Q30353|WD=Q30353|1=824}}), and invited {{ill|lt=Takakamo Shrine|Q11673295|WD=Q11673295|lt=Takakamo no Kami|1=Takakamo Shrine}} from Katsuragi in Yamato, making it the tutelary deity of Kamosho. === Precinct === ファイル:東鴨神社 本殿.JPG|Main hall ファイル:東鴨神社 正面.JPG|Front view ファイル:東鴨神社 鳥居.JPG|Torii === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} Kamo-chō 992 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kamogawa Station|Q5115423|WD=Q5115423|1=Kamogawa Station}} (about 16 minutes on foot) === Nishi Kamo Shrine === {{神社 |名称 = Nishi Kamo Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:西鴨神社 拝殿.JPG|250px]]
